scope.summary: Generates self-contained HTML widgets for MCP Apps protocol hosts, using pasted JSON tool output and optional interactive callServerTool patterns. Widgets are single HTML files that load Fluent UI Web Components and @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps from CDN. The plugin does not itself require or run an MCP server.

externalNetworkCalls: Generated widgets load @fluentui/web-components from unpkg.com CDN
Generated widgets load @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps and @fluentui/tokens from cdn.jsdelivr.net
Generated interactive widgets may call MCP host tools via callServerTool (scoped to the host session)

dependencies.name: MCP host with MCP Apps protocol support
dependencies.description: Generated interactive widgets call MCP host tools via callServerTool. Requires a host that supports the MCP Apps protocol (Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code, Microsoft 365 Copilot).

dependencies.name: CDN access for widget libraries
dependencies.description: Generated widgets load Fluent UI Web Components from unpkg.com and ext-apps/tokens from cdn.jsdelivr.net. CSP rules may need to be updated per environment for all referenced CDNs. If EDS replaces Fluent UI Web Components, CDN dependencies may change.

technologies.name: Model Context Protocol
technologies.usedFor: MCP Apps protocol widgets and interactive tool calls.

technologies.name: Fluent UI Web Components
technologies.usedFor: Generated widget UI components loaded from CDN (v3 beta via unpkg.com).
technologies.source: equinor/techradar checked 2026-05-28 — no blip exists (missing from radar)

technologies.name: Equinor Design System (EDS)
technologies.usedFor: Not currently used. EDS is the preferred frontend design system per Equinor standards but widgets use Fluent UI Web Components instead.

exceptions: Generated widgets currently use Fluent UI Web Components v3 (beta) and custom design-guidelines.md tokens. EDS is preferred per owner decision. Assessment needed: determine whether EDS provides sufficient components or equivalent alternatives to replace Fluent UI Web Components for MCP App widgets.

publication.targetRepository: equinor/powerplatform (recommended if Power Platform scope retained)

blockers: EDS component equivalence assessment needed: determine whether EDS provides sufficient components to replace Fluent UI Web Components for generated widgets.
Fluent UI Web Components is missing from the Equinor Technology Radar — requires architecture discussion path (may become moot if EDS replaces it).
CDN dependency (unpkg.com, cdn.jsdelivr.net) requires CSP rule updates per environment. Dependency scope may change after EDS assessment.

evidence.summary: Documents MCP Apps widget generation, CDN-loaded output, Fluent UI components, callServerTool-capable samples, and 53 eval test cases.
Main skill reviewed. Requires user-provided JSON before generating, uses escapeHtml for XSS prevention, type-safe data handling, correct CDN imports, and accessibility guidance.
Thin wrapper referencing shared report-issue workflow. No security concerns.
Technical reference covering App API, Fluent UI components, CDN loading with UMD collision avoidance, HTML escaping, accessibility, and data type safety patterns.
Visual design defaults using Fluent theme tokens. Covers loading/data/error states, typography, spacing, color tokens, contrast rules, and prefers-reduced-motion.
Read-only sample widget. Uses escapeHtml, theme support, Fluent components. No secrets or external calls beyond CDN.
Interactive sample with callServerTool for refresh. Demonstrates tool calling pattern. No secrets or production endpoints.
MCP: Adopt (2026-05-06). EDS: Adopt. Fluent UI Web Components: missing-from-radar (checked 2026-05-28).

Owner confirmed: @equinor/dcs-power-platform-team owns the plugin; support via #powerplatform Slack. EDS should be preferred — needs assessment whether EDS provides sufficient/equivalent components to replace Fluent UI Web Components. CDN dependency is contingent on EDS outcome; CSP rules need per-environment updates for all referenced CDNs. Copilot CLI and Claude CLI use the same manifest mechanism.

notes: Full review completed 2026-05-28. Skills, samples, and references are reviewed and contain no secrets, no production interaction, and include XSS/accessibility protections. Owner assigned 2026-05-28 (@equinor/dcs-power-platform-team, #powerplatform). EDS preferred — assessment needed for component equivalence vs Fluent UI Web Components. CDN dependency requires per-environment CSP updates. Copilot CLI and Claude CLI use the same manifest (copilotManifest set true). Remaining blockers: EDS assessment, Fluent UI radar status (may become moot), CDN/CSP planning, install test. Plugin eligible for controlled-pilot once EDS assessment is completed and install is tested.
